evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Count to my .NET MAUI app. Here's my empty page: EmptyBarcodeCountPage.xaml + EmptyBarcodeCountPage.xaml.cs, and my MauiProgram: EmptyMauiProgram.cs. I need to count EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes, show the built-in counting UI, and when the user taps the List button store the scanned barcodes so I can show them on a results screen.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit MAUI NuGet versions, updates MauiProgram.cs with .UseScanditCore().UseScanditBarcode(c => c.AddBarcodeCountView()), adds <scandit:BarcodeCountView> with DataCaptureContext and BarcodeCount bindings (and ViewStyle) to the page using the Count.UI.Maui XAML namespace, writes a complete BarcodeCount integration (DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey, a manually-managed Camera via Camera.GetDefaultCamera(BarcodeCount.RecommendedCameraSettings) + SetFrameSourceAsync, new BarcodeCountSettings() enabling only Ean13Upca and Code128, BarcodeCount.Create(context, settings), the Scanned event copying session.RecognizedBarcodes out via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread, ListButtonTapped subscribed in HandlerChanged storing the barcodes, camera SwitchToDesiredStateAsync on/off + barcodeCount.Enabled across OnAppearing/OnDisappearing). Shows the setup checklist (four NuGet packages, NSCameraUsageDescription on iOS, SupportedOSPlatformVersion 24 on Android, license key). Does NOT add ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() calls.

assertions.text: Setup checklist mentions all four NuGet packages: Scandit.DataCapture.Core, Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Maui, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Maui
MauiProgram.cs is updated to call .UseScanditCore() with NO inner configure lambda
MauiProgram.cs calls .UseScanditBarcode( with an inner configure lambda that invokes AddBarcodeCountView()
MauiProgram.cs does NOT call .UseScanditCore(c => c.AddDataCaptureView()) (that is the BarcodeBatch/BarcodeCapture chain, wrong for BarcodeCount)
No ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() or ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() call is added to MainApplication or AppDelegate (the MAUI builder handles this)
The page XAML adds the xmlns:scandit="clr-namespace:Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Count.UI.Maui;assembly=ScanditBarcodeCaptureMaui" namespace
The page XAML contains a <scandit:BarcodeCountView> element
<scandit:BarcodeCountView> sets DataCaptureContext="{Binding DataCaptureContext}" (binding to a DataCaptureContext property)
<scandit:BarcodeCountView> sets BarcodeCount="{Binding BarcodeCount}" (binding to a BarcodeCount property)
The view style is set via the ViewStyle property (e.g. ViewStyle="Icon" or ViewStyle="Dot"), NOT via a Style attribute
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Platforms/iOS/Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions SupportedOSPlatformVersion of at least 24 for the Android target
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
new BarcodeCountSettings() is used (the settings use a plain constructor)
BarcodeCount.Create( is used as the factory (NOT new BarcodeCount(...), which has no public constructor)
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
Kotlin-style underscore symbology names (Symbology.EAN13_UPCA, Symbology.CODE_128) are NOT used, and Swift-style camelCase (.ean13UPCA) is NOT used
A Camera is obtained via Camera.GetDefaultCamera(...) using BarcodeCount.RecommendedCameraSettings
dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync( is called with the camera (the camera is managed explicitly, not by the view)
camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On) and FrameSourceState.Off are used to drive the camera across the lifecycle
barcodeCount.Enabled is set to true so frames are processed
No barcodeCountView.OnAppearing()/OnDisappearing()/StartScanning() calls are made (those are SparkScanView methods; the MAUI BarcodeCountView does not have them)
Either barcodeCount.Scanned += handler or barcodeCount.AddListener(IBarcodeCountListener) is wired
The scan handler reads args.Session.RecognizedBarcodes (the BarcodeCountSession exposes RecognizedBarcodes, not AddedTrackedBarcodes)

The recognized barcodes are copied out of the session (e.g. .ToList()) inside the callback, and UI work is dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread (not RunOnUiThread, not DispatchQueue.MainQueue)
The ListButtonTapped (and/or ExitButtonTapped) event is subscribed inside the barcodeCountView.HandlerChanged handler, not in the constructor before the handler exists
The ListButtonTapped handler stores/shows the scanned barcodes
OnAppearing is overridden and triggers a flow that calls Permissions.CheckStatusAsync<Permissions.Camera>() or Permissions.RequestAsync<Permissions.Camera>()
No Android-only APIs appear (RunOnUiThread, FindViewById, FrameLayout, AppCompatActivity, container.AddView, [Activity])
No BarcodeArView, IBarcodeArHighlightProvider, or BarcodeAr API is used (MatrixScan Count is not MatrixScan AR)
No generic <scandit:DataCaptureView> or BarcodeCaptureOverlay is created (BarcodeCount uses its own <scandit:BarcodeCountView>)

evals.prompt: I'm building a receiving app with MatrixScan Count on .NET MAUI. I already have it counting (see IntegratedBarcodeCountPageViewModel.cs). Now I want to check scanned barcodes against an expected list: 3x of 0123456789012 and 1x of 9876543210987, and react when everything on the list has been scanned.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a capture list: builds TargetBarcode.Create(data, quantity) entries, creates a BarcodeCountCaptureList.Create(listener, targets), implements IBarcodeCountCaptureListListener (OnObservationStarted/OnObservationStopped/OnCaptureListSessionUpdated/OnCaptureListCompleted), and applies it via barcodeCount.SetBarcodeCountCaptureList(list), preserving existing integration.

assertions.text: TargetBarcode.Create(data, quantity) is used to build the expected barcodes (factory, not new TargetBarcode(...))
A TargetBarcode for 0123456789012 with quantity 3 and one for 9876543210987 with quantity 1 are created
BarcodeCountCaptureList.Create(listener, targets) is used (factory, not new BarcodeCountCaptureList(...))
IBarcodeCountCaptureListListener is implemented with OnObservationStarted, OnObservationStopped, OnCaptureListSessionUpdated, and OnCaptureListCompleted
barcodeCount.SetBarcodeCountCaptureList(list) is called to apply the list
OnCaptureListCompleted is where the all-scanned reaction is handled
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, Scanned handler, ResumeAsync/SleepAsync) is preserved
No Android- or iOS-only APIs are introduced (RunOnUiThread, DispatchQueue.MainQueue, FindViewById)

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration in my MatrixScan Count .NET MAUI app — silent counting. Update the feedback in IntegratedBarcodeCountPageViewModel.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCount.Feedback to a new BarcodeCountFeedback() (empty constructor = silent), imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Count.Feedback, preserving the rest of the integration. It does NOT call DefaultFeedback as a method.

assertions.text: barcodeCount.Feedback is reassigned to a new BarcodeCountFeedback() (empty constructor)
BarcodeCountFeedback is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Count.Feedback
BarcodeCountFeedback.DefaultFeedback is NOT called as a method (with parentheses) — it is a static property
The feedback slots are referred to as Success / Failure if mentioned (NOT Scanned / Tapped, which are MatrixScan AR's)
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, Scanned handler, ResumeAsync/SleepAsync) is preserved
